items.question_text: Complete: Helicopter and powered-lift taxi and ground movement operations may be conducted on the movement area provided the helicopter or powered-lift maintains a specific _____ with the surface.
items.explanation: Helicopters may air taxi, hover taxi, or surface taxi, each with different relationships to the surface.

items.question_text: Under 3-11-2, helicopter takeoff clearances may be issued from locations other than the _____.
items.explanation: Helicopters may be cleared for takeoff from locations other than the runway — they don't need runway surfaces.

items.question_text: Under 3-11-3, helicopter departure separation may be reduced because helicopters are not subject to the same _____ constraints as fixed-wing aircraft.
items.explanation: Helicopters have unique wake turbulence characteristics and operational flexibility that allow different separation standards.

items.question_text: Under 3-11-4, helicopter arrival separation may differ from fixed-wing separation because helicopters can operate at lower speeds and with _____ landing approaches.
items.explanation: Helicopters can execute steeper approaches and operate at lower speeds, allowing different separation standards.

items.question_text: Under 3-11-5, simultaneous helicopter landings or takeoffs on the same movement area require what minimum condition?
items.explanation: Simultaneous helicopter operations require that the operations do not adversely affect each other. Visual or physical separation must be maintained.

choices.text: Operations must not adversely affect other aircraft.
One helicopter must be twice the size of the other.
Both helicopters must be of the same military service.
Operations may only be conducted between sunrise and sunset.

items.question_text: Under 3-11-6, a helicopter landing clearance may be issued to a location other than a runway, but what must the controller consider?
items.explanation: When issuing a landing clearance to a non-runway location, the controller must ensure the landing area is suitable and that the operation will not conflict with other traffic on the movement area.

choices.text: Whether the helicopter has filed an IFR flight plan.
Whether the designated landing area is suitable and the operation will not conflict with other traffic.
Whether the helicopter pilot has logged at least 100 hours in the aircraft type.
Whether the helicopter is equipped with a functioning TCAS.
